Jerusalem is the city of our God: no one on earth gives him proper honor except the citizens of the spiritual Jerusalem. Happy is the kingdom, the city, the family, the heart, where God is great, where he is all. There God is known. The more clearly we understand the Lord and his greatness, the more it is expected that we will abound in praise. The earth is, by sin, covered with deformity; therefore, it was right that this place, which was beautified with holiness, be called the joy of the whole earth—that which the whole earth has reason to rejoice in, that God would truly dwell with people on earth. The kings of the earth were afraid of it. Nothing in nature more aptly represents the overthrow of paganism by the Spirit of the gospel than a fleet wrecked in a storm. Both are by the mighty power of the Lord.

Here is the improvement which the people of God should make of his glorious and gracious appearances for them. Let our faith in the word of God be confirmed by this. Let our hope for the stability of the church be encouraged. Let our minds be filled with good thoughts of God. All the streams of mercy that flow to us must be traced to the fountain of his loving-kindness. Let us give to God the glory of the great things he has done for us. Let all members of the church take comfort from what the Lord does for his church. Let us observe the beauty, strength, and safety of the church. Consider its strength; see it founded on Christ the Rock, fortified by divine power, guarded by him who neither slumbers nor sleeps. See what precious ordinances are its palaces, what precious promises are its bulwarks, so that you may be encouraged to join yourselves to it: and tell this to others. This God, who has now done such great things for us, is unchangeable in his love for us and his care for us. If he is our God, he will lead and keep us even to the end. He will guide us so that we are above the reach of death, so that it will not do us any real harm. He will lead us to a life where there will be no more death.
